# Example repository?

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Curious if there ia an example repository for the agentic model used in this repository besides this one. I didn't see one but was curious.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

It might be interesting to see how it works against a different repository, as it seems somewhat coupled to this one at the moment.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

None, just curious.

## Timeline

- 2026-07-15T02:27:25Z @LrWm3 added the `enhancement` label

### @neo-gpt-emmy - 2026-07-15T14:25:54Z

Thanks for asking. Your read is fair at the **public onboarding surface**: Neo's public self-hosting proof is concentrated in this monorepo, which can make the Agent OS look more repository-coupled than it is.

The important boundary is:

- **The Brain / Agent OS is repository-agnostic.** Knowledge Base ingestion, Memory Core, the Native Edge Graph, MCP tooling, peer coordination, and the review workflow can run around an arbitrary repository. A LOCAL deployment runs beside one developer's checkout; a CLOUD deployment provides tenant-scoped ingestion and shared institutional memory around a team's repositories.
- **The Body is Neo's multi-threaded application engine.** It is one possible product/runtime the Brain can maintain.
- **Neural Link is deliberately Neo-specific today.** It lets agents inspect and mutate a live Neo application. Understanding a Rust, Python, Java, or TypeScript repository does not require Neural Link; that path uses ingestion + Knowledge Base + Memory Core + graph.

There is not yet a separate **public, end-to-end example repository** I can link that demonstrates the full Brain operating around an unrelated target codebase. The closest public references today are:

- [What Neo is: Body + Brain + Neural Link](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/benefits/Introduction.md)
- [The Agent OS on Your Codebase](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/benefits/brain/AgentOSOnYourCodebase.md)
- [Day-0 Cloud Deployment](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/Day0Tutorial.md)
- [Runnable cloud-ingestion extension examples](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/tree/dev/examples/cloud-deployment)

I think this issue identifies a real missing proof surface: a small, ordinary target repository plus the configuration/ingestion hooks and an annotated first-run journey would make the portability much easier to evaluate than architecture prose alone.

The active [v13.2 roadmap](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/ROADMAP.md) is closing the adjacent onboarding gap: download and run the local harness without hand-editing config, then start and steer agents through the Fleet Manager cockpit rather than a terminal. I suggest keeping this issue open and scoping the standalone public example against that supported onboarding path instead of documenting today's rough edges as if they were the final UX.

If you would like to help pressure-test that path—or contribute to the eventual example—contributions are very welcome.

— **Emmy (GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra, Codex)**

_Triaged per `ticket-triage`: retained `enhancement`; added `ai` and `documentation`. The six-stage retrospective passes. Leaving this unassigned while the example shape is scoped against the v13.2 onboarding authority._
